name: General IPA Feature Inventory
source: Compiled from Chomsky & Halle (1968), Hayes (2009), Clements & Hume (1995), Ladefoged & Maddieson (1996), and post-2000 research. See accompanying document 'Distinctive Features: Definitions, Evidence, and Justifications v2'.

notes.consonantal: ɹ ɻ ʋ are [+consonantal] per cluster/sonority/syllabic evidence. ɰ ɥ are [-consonantal] (true glides). ʔ is [+consonantal, -sonorant] per Hayes system.
notes.strident: ɬ ɮ are [-strident]. Navajo sibilant harmony shows lateral fricatives are fully transparent — neither trigger nor target — proving they are not sibilants.
notes.implosives: Default [-sonorant] for ɓ ɗ ɠ ʛ. Sande & Oakley (2023) show 38% of languages have implosives pattern with sonorants; language-specific evidence should override.
notes.glottal_stop: ʔ is [+consonantal, -sonorant] (Hayes/SPE analysis). In the Blevins analysis it would be [-consonantal, +sonorant, +ConstrGl]; change if using Blevins framework.
notes.h: h is [-consonantal, +sonorant] (placeless voiceless glide). Harmony transparency data supports [+sonorant] (Walker 2011).
notes.clicks: Click posterior closure is uvular [DORSAL, -high, +back] per Miller et al. (2009a) ultrasound data on N|uu. Laryngeal features (SpreadGl, Voice, Nasal) overlay clicks just as for pulmonic stops. Only tenuis click base forms are listed here.
notes.trill_continuant: Trills and taps are [+continuant] following Hayes (2009). Steriade (1993) Aperture Theory provides compelling arguments for [-continuant] for trills in strong positions; see document for discussion.
notes.ATR: ATR is distinct from tense/lax per Tiede (1996) MRI evidence. [+ATR] applied to canonical [+ATR] vowels in African harmony systems (i e u o and tense counterparts). Needs language-specific calibration.
notes.approximant: [+approximant] marks the class of vowels + glides + liquids (all sonorants except nasals). Optional feature; many analyses express this class as [+sonorant, -nasal].
notes.rhotic_vowel: ɚ (rhotacised schwa) is a vowel with [+coronal] specification from the r-colouring gesture; [CORONAL, -anterior, +distributed] captures the tongue shape.
